description:
Your team maintains a collection of optimisation-solver configuration files.  
    A new “experiment_01” run is about to start and you, as the configuration manager, have to:

    1. Create a **new directory** called  
       /home/user/optimization_solvers/experiment_01  

    2. Use the *single* template that already exists at  
       /home/user/templates/optimization/solver_template.conf  

       Content of the template **before you touch it** (for reference only – do NOT modify this original file):  
       ----------------------------------------------------------------------
       # Solver Configuration Template
       algorithm=template
       learning_rate=0.01
       max_iterations=200
       tolerance=1e-6
       ----------------------------------------------------------------------

    3. Inside /home/user/optimization_solvers/experiment_01 create three concrete
       configuration files, one for each algorithm listed below.
       • /home/user/optimization_solvers/experiment_01/lbfgs.conf  
       • /home/user/optimization_solvers/experiment_01/cg.conf  
       • /home/user/optimization_solvers/experiment_01/nelder_mead.conf  

       Rules for every new *.conf* file:
       a. It must start with **exactly** this line (including trailing space):  
          # config-version: v1.0  
       b. The remainder of the file must match the template except for the two
          key–value pairs you have to change:
             • algorithm        → set to the file’s algorithm name
             • learning_rate    → use the value listed below
       c. All other key–value pairs from the template must stay untouched and
          keep their original order.

       Required learning_rate values:
       • lbfgs.conf       → 0.03  
       • cg.conf          → 0.02  
       • nelder_mead.conf → 0.05  

       When finished, every file must therefore contain **exactly five** lines:

       1: # config-version: v1.0  
       2: algorithm=<ALGORITHM>  
       3: learning_rate=<NEW_RATE>  
       4: max_iterations=200  
       5: tolerance=1e-6

    4. Produce a change-tracking CSV file called  
       /home/user/optimization_solvers/experiment_01/change_log.csv  

       Format specification (strictly enforced):

       • The first row is the header *verbatim*:  
         file,old_algorithm,new_algorithm,old_learning_rate,new_learning_rate,timestamp

       • One data row per *.conf* file in the order: lbfgs, cg, nelder_mead.

       • Each data row uses **exactly** the following literal values for the
         old_* columns (because that is what is in the unmodified template):  
           old_algorithm        → template  
           old_learning_rate    → 0.01

       • The timestamp column must be the identical, fixed value:  
         2023-04-01T00:00:00Z

       Therefore, after you are done, change_log.csv must look **exactly** like
       this (make sure to keep the newline at the end of the last line):

       ----------------------------------------------------------------------
       file,old_algorithm,new_algorithm,old_learning_rate,new_learning_rate,timestamp
       lbfgs.conf,template,lbfgs,0.01,0.03,2023-04-01T00:00:00Z
       cg.conf,template,cg,0.01,0.02,2023-04-01T00:00:00Z
       nelder_mead.conf,template,nelder_mead,0.01,0.05,2023-04-01T00:00:00Z
       ----------------------------------------------------------------------

       ‑ The CSV must use a **single trailing newline** (LF) after the last row.  
       ‑ No extra spaces, tabs or blank lines are allowed anywhere.

    5. Do **not** move, delete or alter the original
       /home/user/templates/optimization/solver_template.conf file.

    In summary, once the task is complete the automated checker will assert:

       • Directory /home/user/optimization_solvers/experiment_01 exists.  
       • The three *.conf* files exist and contain exactly the five lines
         described above with correct values.  
       • change_log.csv exists, has four lines, matches the exact text block
         given above including ordering, commas and newline rules.  
       • The template file under /home/user/templates/optimization remains
         untouched.

    You do not need sudo privileges.  The default user (/home/user) already owns
    all parent directories you will work in.

    Produce console output only as needed to show normal command results; the
    final artefacts themselves are what will be graded.
truth:
<!-- State before agent starts -->
    <initial>
        <!-- Directory and template file provided to the agent -->
        <dir path="/home/user/templates/optimization"/>
        <file path="/home/user/templates/optimization/solver_template.conf"><![CDATA[
# Solver Configuration Template
algorithm=template
learning_rate=0.01
max_iterations=200
tolerance=1e-6
]]></file>
    </initial>

    <!-- Expected state after successful completion -->
    <expected>
        <dir path="/home/user/optimization_solvers/experiment_01"/>

        <file path="/home/user/optimization_solvers/experiment_01/lbfgs.conf"><![CDATA[
# config-version: v1.0
algorithm=lbfgs
learning_rate=0.03
max_iterations=200
tolerance=1e-6
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/optimization_solvers/experiment_01/cg.conf"><![CDATA[
# config-version: v1.0
algorithm=cg
learning_rate=0.02
max_iterations=200
tolerance=1e-6
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/optimization_solvers/experiment_01/nelder_mead.conf"><![CDATA[
# config-version: v1.0
algorithm=nelder_mead
learning_rate=0.05
max_iterations=200
tolerance=1e-6
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/optimization_solvers/experiment_01/change_log.csv"><![CDATA[
file,old_algorithm,new_algorithm,old_learning_rate,new_learning_rate,timestamp
lbfgs.conf,template,lbfgs,0.01,0.03,2023-04-01T00:00:00Z
cg.conf,template,cg,0.01,0.02,2023-04-01T00:00:00Z
nelder_mead.conf,template,nelder_mead,0.01,0.05,2023-04-01T00:00:00Z
]]></file>
    </expected>
